project.name: Anamnesis CLI Guide
project.description: Learn how to use the Anamnesis flashcard tool

cards.back: Validates the project, generates the web interface, and starts a local server (default port 5000). Use -p to specify port and -d for project directory.
Validates all YAML and JSON files, checks required fields, verifies character limits, validates dependencies, and ensures unique card IDs. Use -d flag to specify project directory.
Creates a new Anamnesis project with the specified name. Sets up directories (decks/, build/), creates _project.yml, .gitignore, and an example deck with getting_started content.

metadata.name: Creating Content
metadata.description: Learn how to create decks and cards

cards.back: Each card depends on exactly one other card in the same deck, or null for the first card. Cards cannot reference cards in other decks.

cards.front: What goes in a card .json file?
cards.back: display_name (max 60 chars), front (question, max 200 chars), and back (answer, max 500 chars). The filename becomes the card ID.

cards.front: What goes in a card .yml file?
cards.back: depends_on: single card ID or null. Each card can depend on only one other card in the same deck. One card per deck must have null (the first card).

Decks can depend on multiple other decks using depends_on in _deck.yml. Dependencies must reference existing deck IDs. Decks are automatically sorted topologically - dependencies appear before dependents in all views.

cards.front: What are the size limits for decks?
cards.back: Each deck can contain a maximum of 9 cards. There is no limit on the number of decks in a project. This constraint ensures decks remain focused and manageable.
display_name (human-readable deck name), description (deck summary), and depends_on (list of prerequisite deck IDs). Every deck must have this file.

A horizontal graph showing deck dependencies flowing left-to-right. Dependencies appear on the left, the current deck in the middle, and dependents on the right. Click nodes to navigate, drag to pan the graph.

cards.front: How does Anamnesis order decks and cards?
cards.back: Both decks and cards are automatically sorted topologically based on dependencies. Dependencies always appear before their dependents, ensuring a logical learning progression from foundational to advanced concepts.

Checks: valid YAML/JSON syntax, required fields present, character limits (display_name: 60, front: 200, back: 500), unique card IDs, valid dependencies, exactly one first card per deck, maximum 9 cards per deck, no circular dependencies.

metadata.name: Getting Started
metadata.description: Introduction to Anamnesis and basic concepts

cards.back: Install using pip: pip install open-anamnesis. This gives you access to the anamnesis, init, compile, and build commands.

cards.front: What are the key features of Anamnesis?
cards.back: Dependency management with topological sorting, YAML/JSON validation, interactive lineage graphs, auto-generated web interface with Learn and Test modes, and flashcards-as-code for version control.
Anamnesis is an open-source CLI tool for creating and managing flashcard projects as code. It provides dependency management, validation, and a web-based learning interface.
Contains generated files: manifest.json, index.html, and static/ folder with CSS and JavaScript. Created automatically by anamnesis build.
Contains all deck folders. Each deck is a subdirectory with _deck.yml and card files (.json and .yml pairs).
The root configuration file containing project metadata: name, description, and version. Required in every Anamnesis project. Note: Uses underscore prefix to distinguish it from other files.
